title: Vert Route
description: One curated trail route. It is a standard GeoJSON Feature: 'geometry' holds the path on the map, 'properties' holds everything the recommender and the UI need to know about the route. One file = one route.

type.description: Always the literal string 'Feature'. This is what makes the file valid GeoJSON.

geometry.description: The line on the map that the runner follows.

type.description: A route is a single connected line, so the geometry type is always 'LineString'.

coordinates.description: An ordered list of points along the route. GeoJSON order is [longitude, latitude, elevation] - longitude FIRST (this trips everyone up). Elevation is in meters. Get these by recording the run on a watch/phone and exporting a GPX, then converting; don't hand-type them.

properties.description: Everything about the route that isn't its shape on the map.

id.description: Stable, unique, lowercase-with-hyphens id, e.g. 'tennessee-valley-coastal-loop'. Used in URLs and the database. Never change it once a route is live.

name.description: Human-friendly name shown in the app, e.g. 'Tennessee Valley to Pirates Cove Loop'.

status.description: Editorial state: 'draft' = still being worked on, not shown to users; 'active' = vetted and OK to recommend. Separate from physical trail closures, which are handled by the closures system at recommendation time.

region.description: Which Marin area the route is in. Keep this list short and controlled - add a new value here on purpose, don't free-type.

shape.description: Route shape. 'point-to-point' means it starts and ends in different places (needs a shuttle/2 cars).

distance_km.description: Total distance in kilometers. The ingest script derives this from the geometry and warns if your number is off - so this is really a sanity check on the track.

gain_m.description: Total elevation gain (climbing) in meters - Vert's namesake. Also cross-checked by the ingest script against the geometry.

difficulty.description: Your judgment call, accounting for distance, climb, and how technical the footing is.

surface.description: Rough breakdown of what the runner is on, as percentages. The three should add up to ~100.

vibe_tags.description: Controlled vocabulary of descriptive tags. These ARE the matching signal - thin or inconsistent tags = bad recommendations. Pick from the list; add a new tag to the enum deliberately when you genuinely need one.

lat.description: Latitude of the start (note: lat here, normal order, because it's a labeled field - not a GeoJSON coordinate pair).

parking.description: What parking is like. 'limited' = small lot that fills early on weekends.

notes.description: Optional: 'Lot fills by 9am Sat/Sun', 'Use the overflow lot', etc.

dogs_allowed.description: Optional. Many Marin trails have specific dog rules - worth capturing.

water_on_route.description: Optional. Is there drinkable water (fountain/spigot) along the way?

last_verified.description: Optional. YYYY-MM-DD date you last confirmed the route is accurate and open. Feeds the staleness idea.

founder_notes.description: Your voice - the local-knowledge sentence or two that a generated route can't give. 'Start early to beat the fog burn-off; the Pirates Cove descent is steep and loose.'
